title: CANCOTS 2027
text:
Venue
All conference activities will be held at XXX
Accommodation
Hotel block information and recommended accommodation will be posted here.
Getting to Toronto
By Air
Toronto is served by Toronto Pearson International Airport (YYZ), the largest airport in Canada, with connections from across North America and internationally. Billy Bishop Toronto City Airport (YTZ) also serves select domestic and US routes.
By Train
Via Rail serves Toronto’s Union Station, with connections from Montreal, Ottawa, Québec City, Windsor, and Niagara Falls.
Getting Around Toronto
Toronto has an extensive transit system operated by the TTC (Toronto Transit Commission), including subway, streetcar, and bus routes. The PRESTO card is the easiest way to pay for transit.
Things to Do in Toronto
Toronto offers world-class museums, restaurants, and attractions:

CN Tower — iconic Toronto landmark and observation tower
Royal Ontario Museum — art, culture, and natural history
Art Gallery of Ontario — one of Canada’s largest art museums
Kensington Market — eclectic neighbourhood with food, shops, and street art
St. Lawrence Market — historic market with local vendors
Toronto Islands — ferry ride to parks and beaches with city views

title: CANCOTS 2027
text:
“Conferences On Teaching Statistics” (COTS) have a long and rich history, starting from the earliest such international conferences (International Conference on Teaching Statistics – ICOTS) in 1982 (Sheffield, UK) and 1986 (Victoria, Canada). A total of twelve ICOTS have been held, every four years since 1982, and a number of regional versions, including

OZCOTS (Australia and NZ),
USCOTS (United States),
eCOTS,
UKCOTS (United Kingdom),
CFIES (France, Belgium)

have also been held.
The first edition of CANCOTS was held in Montreal in 2025 (https://cancots.netlify.app/)



  For questions or comments, contact cancots2027@example.com
  © 2027 CANCOTS 2027 Organizing Committee

title: CANCOTS 2027
text:
Introduction
The second Canadian Conference on Teaching Statistics (CanCOTS 2027) will be held at the University of Toronto from Monday June 22nd to Wednesday June 24th, 2027. CanCOTS brings together statistics educators, researchers, and practitioners from across Canada and beyond to share ideas, collaborate, and advance the teaching and learning of statistics. The conference will combine structured sessions with collaborative working time, giving participants the opportunity to both learn from one another and leave with concrete outcomes.
Details on format, schedule, and registration will be announced in the coming months.
Sponsors
We gratefully acknowledge the support and sponsorship of the following organizations:

Department of Statistical Sciences, University of Toronto
CANSSI Ontario
